analysis.summary: Kinjal+Sayan C is favoured (44.4% win vs 35.4% for Raktim, 20.2% draw). Expected goals: Kinjal+Sayan C 2.21 – 1.95 Raktim.

key_factors.explanation: Kinjal+Sayan C converts chances better (finishing 0.97 vs 0.61).
Kinjal+Sayan C has the stronger keeper (0.60 vs 0.30), shifting expected goals against.
Kinjal+Sayan C is more exposed on the counter (transition risk 0.22 vs 0.42 — lower is safer).
Kinjal+Sayan C builds more volume (creation 0.57 vs 0.48).
Kinjal+Sayan C defends better in the back line (0.58 vs 0.48).
Kinjal+Sayan C shields the defence better (mid-def 0.37 vs 0.32).

sections.title: Verdict
paragraphs: Kinjal+Sayan C is favoured (44.4% win vs 35.4% for Raktim, 20.2% draw). Expected goals: Kinjal+Sayan C 2.21 – 1.95 Raktim.

sections.title: Season profiles (prime / pick-season)
paragraphs: Kinjal+Sayan C pick-season: Diego Godín uses 2015-2016 only (stats fully replaced).
Raktim pick-season: Alexis Sánchez uses 2016-2017 only (stats fully replaced).
bullets: Prime player: entire stat line replaced by their best top-league season (2014-15+).
Pick-season player: stats replaced by the chosen season only (not blended with current form).

sections.title: Squad depth (bench impact)
paragraphs: Non-starters with elite per-90 traits add a small squad-depth multiplier to unit ratings (capped at ~5% total; bench cannot dominate outcomes).
Kinjal+Sayan C: 4 on bench; 3 with standout quality (+3.3% squad depth boost). Standouts: Pape Matar Sarr, Bruno Guimarães, Nico O'Reilly. Boosts — attack +0.9%, creation +1.2%, defence +1.2%.
Raktim: 4 on bench; 3 with standout quality (+4.3% squad depth boost). Standouts: Alexis Sánchez, Éder Militão, Antonee Robinson. Boosts — attack +2.0%, creation +1.1%, defence +1.2%.

sections.title: Expected goals pipeline
paragraphs: The engine splits attack into finishing and chance-creation channels (xA / xG-buildup / xG-chain credit all outfield positions), then applies opponent suppression (back line 54%, mid shield 32%, GK 14%) and midfield battle modifiers.
Kinjal+Sayan C raw attack xG: 2.61 finishing + 0.21 creation = 2.82 before suppression. When Raktim defends, suppression factor is 0.799 (DEF 0.48, mid-def 0.32, GK 0.30, transition risk 0.42).
Raktim raw attack xG: 1.97 + 0.18 = 2.14. Kinjal+Sayan C suppression when defending: 0.845 (transition risk 0.22).
Midfield battle multipliers: Kinjal+Sayan C ×1.006, Raktim ×0.994. Net xG edge: Kinjal+Sayan C by 0.26.

sections.title: Defensive structure & transitions
paragraphs: Kinjal+Sayan C xGA suppression: 0.691 (base without transition penalty: 0.787). Raktim: 0.749 (base 0.825).

Kinjal+Sayan C fullbacks: Willian Pacho (RB, exposure 0.12), Jules Koundé (LB, exposure 0.32). Team transition risk 0.218 — high attacking fullbacks increase counter vulnerability when midfield cover is thin.

Raktim fullbacks: Achraf Hakimi (RB, exposure 0.45), Trent Alexander-Arnold (LB, exposure 0.52). Team transition risk 0.417 — high attacking fullbacks increase counter vulnerability when midfield cover is thin.
Opposition wing threat vs leaky fullbacks: Kinjal+Sayan C's wide attack gets a +0.6% xG edge (winger threat 0.46, Raktim transition risk 0.42).

Press vs build-up: Kinjal+Sayan C's press trims Raktim's chance creation by ~9.4% (press 0.54 vs resistance 0.19). Press vs build-up: Raktim's press trims Kinjal+Sayan C's chance creation by ~9.5% (press 0.47 vs resistance 0.12).

sections.title: Formation & squad fit
paragraphs: Kinjal+Sayan C (4-3-3 flat): average formation fit 0.81. Chance creation index 0.49, possession 0.70.
Raktim (4-3-3 flat): average formation fit 0.76. Chance creation index 0.49, possession 0.64.

sections.title: Monte Carlo interpretation
paragraphs: Across 10,000 neutral-venue runs, average scoreline tendency is 2.20–1.97. Both teams score 76.3% of the time; over 2.5 goals 78.3%.
bullets: Trophy boost: Kinjal+Sayan C ×1.057, Raktim ×1.037 (applied in simulation units).
